target.name: RoboTwin
target.founder: Megi Mejdrechová, Ladislav Dvořák, David Polák

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
RoboTwin is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Megi Mejdrechová, Ladislav Dvořák, and David Polák, having raised €2.3M in EIC funding. The comparable universe spans the Robotics & Manufacturing sector, comprising 1 public company (ticker 277810.KQ) and 8 private peers including 1X Technologies, Foundation Robotics, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to this target. The analysis identified 12 same-sector peers but insufficient clustering around RoboTwin's specific characteristics: zero companies matched both seed stage and similar valuation tier, with only one adjacent-stage comparable and zero similar-tier peers identified. The standard requirement of three or more datapoints within narrow filters could not be met. Relying on an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity in robotics subsectors, stage distribution, and technology maturity.

The absence of a robust peer-median result leaves this valuation analysis incomplete. Advancing further would require either: (1) expanding the sample to include less-proximate stage or tier comparables with explicit adjustment factors, (2) leveraging transaction multiples if available from the broader private peer set, or (3) deploying bottom-up methods (revenue multiple, cost-of-capital, sum-of-the-parts). Current data does not support a triangulated range. Key uncertainty centers on whether RoboTwin's specific robotics subsector commands valuations more or less favorable than the median across the 12-company sector landscape.
